description: This figure presents the evolution of dislocation density as a function of equivalent plastic strain for Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel during warm multiaxial forging. The chart plots measured dislocation density values (black squares) against a predicted saturation curve (red dashed line) generated using Eq. (7). The vertical axis denotes dislocation density in m^-2, while the horizontal axis represents equivalent plastic strain up to 2.5. Experimental data reveal a rapid initial increase in dislocation density that gradually saturates at higher strain levels. The predicted curve follows the exponential relation rho = 7.96E14 + k1*(1-exp(-k2*epsilon)), with fitted constants k1 = 7.90516E15 +/- 5.28171E14 m^-2 and k2 = 1.81319 +/- 0.42469, yielding an R-square of 0.98554. The caption states that the initial density rho_0 corresponds to the unstrained MAF-0 condition. This figure supports the core conclusion that dislocation multiplication and saturation during warm deformation are accurately captured by the exponential model, furnishing essential parameters for the modified Johnson-Cook constitutive description.
